symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden painless massive haematochezia (bright red blood with clots)
symptoms.character: large volume bright red blood with clots, no pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Painless brisk bright red rectal bleeding is the hallmark of diverticular haemorrhage.
Ischaemic colitis bleeding is preceded by severe abdominal pain, absent here.

symptoms.symptom_name: Near-syncope, weakness, diaphoresis worse on sitting up

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postural near-syncope indicates significant intravascular volume depletion from haemorrhage.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of abdominal pain, cramps, tenesmus and haematemesis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe pain typically precedes ischaemic colitis bleeding.
No haematemesis or melaena; bright red blood favours a lower/colonic source.
Antiplatelet therapy increases severity and reduces spontaneous cessation of GI bleeding.
Pre-existing diverticulosis is the anatomical substrate for a diverticular bleed.
Objective hypotension with compensatory tachycardia is measured shock — sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers shock from reported near-syncope and pallor, not a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Delayed capillary refill (>4 s), pallor, diaphoresis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Peripheral hypoperfusion confirms shock; pallor/diaphoresis are partly video-observable but capillary refill requires hands-on assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Digital rectal examination — copious fresh bright red blood and clots in the rectal vault, no palpable mass or haemorrhoidal source

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a distal haemorrhoidal source on DRE points to a proximal colonic bleed — a clinician-performed examination the AI cannot do.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, non-tender, non-distended abdomen with active bowel sounds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Soft non-tender abdomen argues against an acute inflammatory or ischaemic abdomen; requires palpation the AI cannot perform.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count, coagulation profile, group and crossmatch
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantify blood loss, guide transfusion and correct coagulopathy (relevant given aspirin) — hospital pathology, not obtainable in telehealth.
Localise active arterial bleeding and enable angiographic embolisation in the unstable patient.
Identify and treat the bleeding source and exclude neoplasm once resuscitated.
